title: What Modern History Actually Is
body:
Modern history covers the period when industrial power, nation states, mass politics, and global capitalism became dominant. It begins with revolutionary upheavals and ends with the digital, networked world.

The era is defined by acceleration: steam, electricity, rail, factories, telegraphy, photography, radio, aviation, computers, and the internet all changed what societies could do and how fast they could do it.

Modern history is therefore not only a catalogue of events. It is the story of systems changing shape under pressure from technology, ideology, and empire.

It is the era in which the modern world was built and broken repeatedly.
keyPoints: Industrialisation transformed work and urban life
Nation states and mass politics became dominant
Empire linked distant regions in unequal ways
Modern history is still contested and politically loaded
furtherReading: The Dawn of Everything by David Graeber and David Wengrow
